title: docs: add contributor guide for the pending-issues JSON workflow
body:
## Issue description

The `pending-issues/` directory is the sole mechanism for creating GitHub issues in this repository. Agents and contributors write a JSON file into that directory on a feature branch, open a PR to `main`, and the `process-issue-requests.yml` workflow converts each JSON file into a real GitHub issue and then deletes the file.

There is currently no standalone, human-readable document that explains:

- **What the JSON file must look like** (field names, types, valid values, and which fields are required vs. optional)
- **Where the file must be committed** (branch vs. directory vs. naming convention)
- **The end-to-end workflow** a contributor must follow to get an issue created without triggering mistakes that cause the workflow to skip or fail

The information exists only inside `docs/issue-agent-instructions.md`, which is written for the Issue Agent custom agent, not for human contributors. The `README.md` and `docs/architecture/` documents do not mention the workflow at all.

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. **New document** — create `docs/contributing/issue-submission.md` (or an equally obvious path) that is discoverable from `README.md`.
2. **JSON structure sample** — the document must include a complete, copy-pasteable JSON sample covering all supported fields:
   ```json
   {
     "title": "Short, descriptive issue title",
     "body": "Full issue body in Markdown.\n\nUse \\n for newlines inside this JSON string.",
     "labels": ["enhancement"],
     "assignees": [],
     "milestone": null
   }
   ```
3. **Field reference table** — document every field with name, type, required/optional, and valid values or constraints.
4. **File naming convention** — explain that files must follow `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<short-slug>.json` and be placed inside `pending-issues/`.
5. **Step-by-step workflow** — cover the full process: create a feature branch → write the JSON file → commit → open a PR to `main` → the workflow fires on merge → the file is auto-deleted.
6. **Failure modes** — document what causes the workflow to skip a file (missing `title`, invalid JSON) so contributors can self-diagnose.
7. **Link from README** — `README.md` must include a link to the new document so it is discoverable.
8. **Four-section issue body reminder** — the document must note the recommended four-section body structure (Issue description, Requirements, Additional notes, Acceptance criteria) that all issues in this repository follow.

---

## Additional notes

- The workflow source is `.github/workflows/process-issue-requests.yml`. Refer to it for the authoritative field-handling logic (lines 57–82) when writing the field reference.
- The existing agent instructions in `docs/issue-agent-instructions.md` can be used as a source of truth for the workflow steps, but the new document must be written for human contributors, not AI agents.
- Do **not** modify `.github/workflows/process-issue-requests.yml` or any other workflow file as part of this issue.
- The `pending-issues/.gitkeep` file must remain so that the directory survives Git even when all JSON files have been cleaned up by the workflow.
- Keep the document concise — contributors need a quick reference, not a tutorial.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A new Markdown document exists at `docs/contributing/issue-submission.md` (or equivalent discoverable path).
- [ ] The document includes a complete, valid JSON sample with all five fields (`title`, `body`, `labels`, `assignees`, `milestone`).
- [ ] The document contains a field reference table listing required vs. optional status and valid values for each field.
- [ ] The document states the required file naming convention (`YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<short-slug>.json`) and the required directory (`pending-issues/`).
- [ ] The document describes the end-to-end workflow: branch → write JSON → commit → PR → merge → auto-create → auto-delete.
- [ ] The document lists at least two failure modes that cause the workflow to skip a file.
- [ ] The document mentions the recommended four-section issue body structure.
- [ ] `README.md` contains a link to the new document under a relevant section (e.g. "Contributing" or "Workflow").
- [ ] No workflow files are added or modified.
- [ ] The `pending-issues/.gitkeep` file is unchanged.
